turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Hi Anmol, how are you?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Yeah, hi, I am fine.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Yeah, thank you, thank you Anmol for joining on time. Uh, so we can start now. Uh, firstly introduce about yourself and your education background first, after that your experience.

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Yeah, sure. So I have done B.Tech from Delhi from IP University, and after that I had joined HCL Tech. And in there I have been around for 2.5 years, around that time I had been there. So, and in that I had worked with the technologies mostly related to front end with the React.js and JavaScript along with the HTML and CSS regarding for the design and Tailwind as a library. And after that I moved through to one of the other organization, normal startup. So in that, currently I am working in a project related to EdTech, uh, related to assessment platform. In assessment platform where the students give test regarding that. So I have been doing work on that, and that platform is also on the React.js and JavaScript basically. In that In that I have been doing the work.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: It is brief. Okay.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: As I show your resume, you have MERN stack developer.

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Yeah, in the HCL and here little bit backend I have worked, but major is on frontend, minor is backend.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: So you are looking for frontend developer as of now.

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Majorly I am looking in front end, but also want to go on towards the full stack if I get.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay, then, then I will check back, uh, backend knowledge also in this interview. Is it fine for you?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Or a little bit preparation is less for the backend?

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: No problem. Generally I will ask normal question itself. Uh, for front end I will ask based on your experience. So how much experience you have as of now?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Around 3 years.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay, around 3 years. So I, I can ask front-end interview around 3-year experience student like this, and for backend I will consider you as a fresher. Okay, let's start with the first JavaScript question. After that we will move to the React. Okay, explain event loop and microtask queue and call stack.

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Yes, sure. So event loop has been introduced in JavaScript to firstly handle the synchronous and asynchronous tasks in a sim— in a same program, same one. So what happens, there have been two major queues has been in advance with the implemented by the JavaScript. One is microtask queue and one another is macro task queue. So what happens, the, there are timers which we generally use, setTimeouts, and that, so that goes into the macro task queue, or we can say timer queue. And another is the promises, like we generally use promises for resolving for API purposes and other side purposes. So there is that it is created with the promise queue, or we can say microtask queue. So what happens in the starting of the program? Firstly, all the synchronous tasks has been solved by the JavaScript, and after that, turn comes for the microtask queue, or we can say promise queue. And so firstly synchronous task go to the call stack and that get printed first. Then in the second time microtask queue goes to the call stack. When the synchronous task has been resolved, then microtask queue to the call stack. And in the last, the micro— macro task queue then go to the call stack and then it gets printed. So this whole loop of Managing synchronous and asynchronous task is known as an event loop.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Uh, which queue is higher priority, microtask queue or macro task queue?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Microtask queue has a higher priority than macro task queue.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay, and setTimeout lies on which queue, microtask queue or macro task queue?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: Macro, macro.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Yes, correct. Okay, uh, let's move to the next question. Uh, What are the WeakMap and WeakSet?

turns.speaker: CANDIDATE
turns.text: I have not worked in that WeakMap, so I not remember.
